methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: QuantWare

data.narrative:
PlanQC is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Alexander Glaetzle, evaluated against a comp universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion. The company has raised $50M+ to date.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable method, extrapolates forward from the $50M total raised using a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding a $100M implied valuation. Peer-median comparison was not viable; while 7 same-sector peers exist in the broader quantum computing universe, insufficient narrow-filter matches (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) precluded a statistically defensible median—fewer than the required 3 datapoints qualified under these criteria.

The triangulated range is $85M to $115M, with a $100M median. This valuation hinges on the stage-progression multiplier assumption and lacks cross-validation from comparable transaction or revenue multiples due to the sparse peer set at PlanQC's specific stage and scale. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiplier for quantum hardware companies transitioning from Series A to later rounds, and whether relative positioning within the competitive frontier-tech landscape warrants adjustment from the median estimate.
